mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate supply capacity meeting underlying demand, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over a 5-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during the specified tightening regime, or the forward return differential between tightening and easing periods falls below 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income and consumer credit availability stimulate aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate sales and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly exceed the market average during periods of rising demand indicators.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales and consumer credit signals weakening demand that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor market signal fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return differential of at least 100 basis points over a 4-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion environment, a sustained drop in industrial production below its historical trend signals supply chain bottlenecks and demand exhaustion, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive or statistically neutral during the specified supply contraction regime, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 2-year walk-forward test.
